commands.review_summary:
The PR changes embedded agent subscription streaming to strip ordinary non-phase assistant deltas incrementally while preserving full-buffer recompute for split reasoning/final tags and fenced-code edge cases, with focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +187, Tests +208. Total +395 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection and the added focused tests give a high-confidence reproduction path: current main strips the accumulated deltaBuffer for ordinary non-phase chunks, while the PR test asserts chunk-local stripping and suffix emission. I did not run the tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human maintainer handling is required because this draft, protected PR changes core assistant message delivery and needs landing judgment plus broad proof rather than an automated repair.

target.title: fix(agents): stream assistant deltas incrementally
target.body:
## Summary

- stream ordinary non-phase assistant text deltas incrementally instead of stripping the accumulated `deltaBuffer` on every chunk
- keep full recompute behavior for split block-tag fragments, reasoning-close replacements, and fenced-code literals where chunk-local stripping is unsafe
- carry visible fenced-code state incrementally without letting stripped reasoning fences poison visible parsing
- add regressions for suffix streaming, split orphan close replacement, media-only replacement, hidden-code safety, split fence markers, and backtick/tilde fenced code

Fixes part of https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/markdown/fences.ts src/markdown/code-spans.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.types.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/markdown/fences.ts src/markdown/code-spans.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.types.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: repeated subscriber work over accumulated streamed text during normal non-phase assistant deltas.

Real environment tested: local focused Vitest/static checks in a Codex worktree on Node, after rebasing onto current `origin/main`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: commands listed in Verification.

Evidence after fix: focused tests pass with 4 files / 158 tests; autoreview reports no accepted/actionable findings.

Observed result after fix: ordinary text deltas append from the new chunk, while split tag, orphan close, hidden reasoning, and fenced-code cases still avoid leaking reasoning or stripping literal code content.

What was not tested: current SHA has not yet had a fresh broad Crabbox/Testbox `check:changed`; existing AWS lanes were already active, so the broad gate is queued after they clear.
